assertions.description: The SPI bus entry (bus_id '0') lists only SCK (SCLK) and MISO signals, missing the MOSI signal on net MOSI/SDA (U1 pin 7, SDI/SDA). The MOSI/SDA net connects J3 pins 7/8 to U1 SDI/SDA and has R2 pull-up to VDD. A complete SPI detection should include all three of SCK, MISO, and MOSI. A second spurious bus entry (bus_id 'pin_U1') incorrectly labels the MISO net as MOSI.

The MS5611-01BA supports both I2C and SPI. The schematic uses net names 'MOSI/SDA' (with R2=5.1k pull-up to VDD) and 'SCLK' (with R1=5.1k pull-up to VDD), which are classic I2C SDA and SCL lines with standard pull-up resistors. The global label 'MOSI{slash}SDA' explicitly names the data line as SDA. Despite this, design_analysis.bus_analysis.i2c is empty []. The I2C bus topology should be detected with SDA=MOSI/SDA net, SCL=SCLK net, and pull-ups R1+R2.

D1 (BZT52H-C3V6,115) is a 3.6V Zener diode with cathode on VDD and anode on GND, forming a voltage clamp / ESD protection circuit on the power rail. The signal_analysis.protection_devices array is empty []. This is a clearly identifiable protection device — a Zener diode connected between a power rail and GND is a standard overvoltage clamp.
